text.title: rust-tokio-async Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-tokio-async as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Wrapping sqlx::PgPool or reqwest::Client in Mutex<T>.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both types are already Send + Sync (they use internal Arc-wrapped pools). Adding a Mutex serializes all access, defeating the pool's purpose.

failure-mode.description: tokio::spawn with a closure that captures &Database or any non-'static reference.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: tokio::spawn requires Send + 'static. Agents fix this by adding Arc::new everywhere; review for over-Arc'd state that should be owned or passed by value.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Blocks the Tokio executor thread, starving all other tasks on that thread. Strictly forbidden inside async code. Use .await instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JoinHandle returns Result with JoinError on panic. Discarding it swallows panics silently. Use .await?? or explicitly match the JoinError.

failure-mode.description: Mixing async-std::task::spawn and tokio::spawn in the same project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Different runtimes; futures from one cannot be awaited by the other. Pin Tokio as the single runtime in the system prompt.
